<details><summary><code>client.dcim.<a href="src/fern/dcim/client.py">connected_device_list</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

This endpoint allows a user to determine what device (if any) is connected to a given peer device and peer
interface. This is useful in a situation where a device boots with no configuration, but can detect its neighbors
via a protocol such as LLDP. Two query parameters must be included in the request:

* `peer_device`: The name of the peer device
* `peer_interface`: The name of the peer interface
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from fern import FernApi

client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
client.dcim.connected_device_list(
    peer_device="peer_device",
    peer_interface="peer_interface",
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**peer_device:** `str` — The name of the peer device
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**peer_interface:** `str` — The name of the peer interface
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

```python
client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
```

```python
    device_role=1,
    device_type=1,
    site=1,
)
```

```python
)
```

<details><summary><code>client.dcim.<a href="src/fern/dcim/client.py">interfaces_trace</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Trace a complete cable path and return each segment as a three-tuple of (termination, cable, termination).
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>

<dl>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from fern import FernApi

client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
client.dcim.interfaces_trace(
    id=1,
)

```

</dd>
</dl>

</dd>
</dl>

<dd>

</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>

<details><summary><code>client.dcim.<a href="src/fern/dcim/client.py">power_feeds_trace</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

</dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from fern import FernApi

)
```

</dd>

<details><summary><code>client.extras.<a href="src/fern/extras/client.py">content_types_read</a>(...)</code></summary>

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
)
```

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>

<dl>
<dd>

</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**created:** `typing.Optional[dt.datetime]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**data_type:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**default:** `typing.Optional[typing.Dict[str, typing.Optional[typing.Any]]]` — Default value for the field (must be a JSON value). Encapsulate strings with double quotes (e.g. "Foo").
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**description:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**display:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**filter_logic:** `typing.Optional[WritableCustomFieldFilterLogic]` — Loose matches any instance of a given string; exact matches the entire field.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**group_name:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Custom fields within the same group will be displayed together
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `typing.Optional[int]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**label:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Name of the field as displayed to users (if not provided, the field's name will be used)
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**last_updated:** `typing.Optional[dt.datetime]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**object_type:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**required:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If true, this field is required when creating new objects or editing an existing object.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**search_weight:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Weighting for search. Lower values are considered more important. Fields with a search weight of zero will be ignored.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**type:** `typing.Optional[WritableCustomFieldType]` — The type of data this custom field holds
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**ui_visibility:** `typing.Optional[WritableCustomFieldUiVisibility]` — Specifies the visibility of custom field in the UI
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**url:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**validation_maximum:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Maximum allowed value (for numeric fields)
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**validation_minimum:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Minimum allowed value (for numeric fields)
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**validation_regex:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Regular expression to enforce on text field values. Use ^ and $ to force matching of entire string. For example, <code>^[A-Z]{3}$</code> will limit values to exactly three uppercase letters.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**weight:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Fields with higher weights appear lower in a form.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>

<details><summary><code>client.extras.<a href="src/fern/extras/client.py">custom_links_create</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>


</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from fern import FernApi

client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
client.extras.custom_links_create(
    content_types=["content_types"],
    link_text="link_text",
    link_url="link_url",
    name="name",
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**content_types:** `typing.Sequence[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**link_text:** `str` — Jinja2 template code for link text
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**link_url:** `str` — Jinja2 template code for link URL
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**name:** `str` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dd>

</dd>
</dl>

<dd>

</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**group_name:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Links with the same group will appear as a dropdown menu
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `typing.Optional[int]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**last_updated:** `typing.Optional[dt.datetime]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**new_window:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Force link to open in a new window
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**url:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**weight:** `typing.Optional[int]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

```python
from fern import FernApi

client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",

    name="name",
```
```python
    template_code="template_code",
)
```

</dd>

**id:** `typing.Optional[int]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**last_updated:** `typing.Optional[dt.datetime]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<details><summary><code>client.extras.<a href="src/fern/extras/client.py">webhooks_create</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>


</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from fern import FernApi

client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
client.extras.webhooks_create(
    content_types=["content_types"],
    name="name",
    payload_url="payload_url",
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**content_types:** `typing.Sequence[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**name:** `str` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**payload_url:** `str` — This URL will be called using the HTTP method defined when the webhook is called. Jinja2 template processing is supported with the same context as the request body.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**additional_headers:** `typing.Optional[str]` — User-supplied HTTP headers to be sent with the request in addition to the HTTP content type. Headers should be defined in the format <code>Name: Value</code>. Jinja2 template processing is supported with the same context as the request body (below).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**body_template:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Jinja2 template for a custom request body. If blank, a JSON object representing the change will be included. Available context data includes: <code>event</code>, <code>model</code>, <code>timestamp</code>, <code>username</code>, <code>request_id</code>, and <code>data</code>.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**ca_file_path:** `typing.Optional[str]` — The specific CA certificate file to use for SSL verification. Leave blank to use the system defaults.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**conditions:** `typing.Optional[typing.Dict[str, typing.Optional[typing.Any]]]` — A set of conditions which determine whether the webhook will be generated.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**created:** `typing.Optional[dt.datetime]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**display:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**http_content_type:** `typing.Optional[str]` — The complete list of official content types is available <a href="https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml">here</a>.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**http_method:** `typing.Optional[WebhookHttpMethod]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `typing.Optional[int]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**last_updated:** `typing.Optional[dt.datetime]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**secret:** `typing.Optional[str]` — When provided, the request will include a 'X-Hook-Signature' header containing a HMAC hex digest of the payload body using the secret as the key. The secret is not transmitted in the request.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**ssl_verification:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Enable SSL certificate verification. Disable with caution!
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**type_create:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Call this webhook when a matching object is created.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**type_delete:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Call this webhook when a matching object is deleted.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**type_update:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Call this webhook when a matching object is updated.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**url:** `typing.Optional[str]` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>

```python
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
```

```python
    id_=1,
```
```python
    group_id=1,
    protocol=FhrpGroupProtocol.VRRP2,
)

```

<dd>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from fern import FernApi, WritableL2VpnType

client = FernApi(
    api_key="YOUR_API_KEY",
)
client.ipam.l2vpns_create(
    name="name",
    slug="slug",
    type=WritableL2VpnType.VPWS,
)
```
